attacking_style: Pursues solo efforts when given freedom, but shows tactical timing issues—willing to chase down moves on final climbs but can misjudge the timing required to close gaps.
team_tactics: Typically part of a controlling team structure (Deceuninck-QuickStep), but receives selective freedom on stages where team priorities shift to overall classification defense rather than stage hunting.
terrain_preference: Capable on punchy final climbs that allow pursuit efforts, though not a pure climber. Best suited to stages where strength and persistence matter more than explosive acceleration.
vulnerability: Timing judgment on solo chase efforts—demonstrated ability to make the physical effort but positioned attack too late to succeed on final climb pursuit.
key_patterns: Receives freedom only when team priorities shift away from stage control to GC defense.
Commits to solo pursuit efforts rather than waiting for group dynamics or positioning.
Shows physical capability on climbs but tactical timing lags behind effort capacity.
form_trajectory: neutral: Single observation shows physical capability but tactical execution needs refinement; insufficient data for trend assessment
